response:
the area of the regular hexagonal community center is found by dividing the shape into six equilateral triangles with side lengths of thirty meters where the area of one triangle equals the square root of three over four times the side squared resulting in approximately three hundred eighty nine point seven one square meters per triangle so multiplying this by six yields a total hexagonal area of approximately two thousand three hundred thirty eight point two six square meters using the formula involving the apothem and perimeter confirms this geometric calculation through trigonometric identities relating to the internal angles of one hundred twenty degrees.

the radius of the circular garden corresponds to the apothem of the hexagon which is the distance from the center to the midpoint of a side calculated as thirty times the square root of three divided by two yielding approximately twenty five point nine eight meters and using this radius the area of the circle is computed by squaring the radius and multiplying by pi resulting in approximately two thousand one hundred twenty one point three two square meters while the circumference touching the midpoints ensures the circle is inscribed perfectly within the hexagonal boundary.
